title: Remote Access and Network Management Paths · Packet Forensics
summary: Provide secure operator, vendor, and user connectivity with recoverable in-band and out-of-band paths. Learners isolate plausible failures, compare competing hypotheses, and record cause-level repair evidence. Planned artifact: Repair a changed failure in a least-privilege management access plan and publish the reproduction, evidence, repair, and regression record. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Compare site-to-site and client-to-site VPNs, clientless access, authentication, authorization, and route scope.
Choose split or full tunnel by traffic path, inspection, performance, privacy, DNS, and local-network risk.
Choose SSH, GUI, API, or console management with encrypted transport, identity, least privilege, and logging.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.shell:
# comptia-network-plus safe operations evidence notebook
# Commands run only in the deterministic browser simulator.
# Add one observation, command, expected signal, and conclusion per activity.

options.text: The Net Site Client Vpn record says a complete first version of a least-privilege management access plan while every earlier competency remains active looks right but supplies no governing condition, observation, changed case, or counterexample. This does not satisfy Net Site Client Vpn for a complete first version of a least-privilege management access plan while every earlier competency remains active.
The record names the assumption, applies Net Site Client Vpn, and reports a changed-case check tied to the stakeholder decision.
